topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp snap of the leather wallet against the pavement was the last thing I needed. I stared down at the thick wad of bills, the sheer volume of it making my stomach clench with a sour, hot feeling. It was obscene. Someone had been careless enough to leave this treasure lying around. My jaw tightened until my teeth ached, and I shoved the wallet deep into my pocket, the fabric protesting the sudden tension. I didn't even bother looking for an address. I just needed to get away from this scene, from the implication that I was now somehow connected to this windfall. A low, ragged exhale escaped my lips as I started walking, each step deliberate and fueled by a simmering resentment.

[story 2]

My hands were shaking as I picked up the worn leather. The weight of the cash was staggering, a physical manifestation of unfairness pressing down on my chest. Every nerve ending felt frayed, buzzing with a sharp, unwelcome energy. "Unbelievable," I muttered under my breath, the sound rough and tight in my throat. I wanted to kick the thing, to make the money vanish, but I kept my hands steady, forcing myself to observe the scene with a cold, hard glare. The sheer audacity of it—someone just dropped this—set my teeth on edge. I pocketed it quickly, the paper crinkling loudly, and turned abruptly, my pace quickening as I fled the vicinity.

[story 3]

I found the wallet half-buried near the bus stop, and the sight of the bundled currency hit me like a physical blow. My breath hitched, shallow and fast. A furious heat rose up my neck, making my skin prickle. This wasn't just money; it felt like a deliberate slight, a blatant disregard for others. I clenched my fists so tightly my knuckles turned white. "This is ridiculous," I hissed to the empty air, the words laced with venom. I didn't wait to see if anyone else noticed. With a sharp, jerky movement, I scooped up the contents and spun around, my entire body radiating a coiled, restless energy as I hurried down the street.
